useCases.title: When Do You Need to Convert AVIF to JPG?

items.title: Universal Compatibility
items.description: JPG is supported on every device, OS, and app. Convert AVIF to JPG to share photos on Windows, older iPhones, or apps that don't yet support the AVIF format.

items.title: Email & Messaging
items.description: Most email clients and messaging apps handle JPG without issue. Converting ensures your recipients can view photos without needing to install anything extra.

items.title: Design & Print Workflows
items.description: Many design tools and print services accept JPG but not AVIF. Converting lets you drop images directly into Photoshop, Canva, Word, or any print template.

items.title: CMS & Platform Uploads
items.description: Some content management systems and social platforms don't accept AVIF yet. Converting to JPG ensures smooth uploads to WordPress, Instagram, and similar platforms.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Is it safe to convert private photos here?
faq.a1: Yes. Your files are converted locally in your browser and are never uploaded to any server. No one else can access your images — not even us.
faq.q2: Does converting AVIF to JPG lose quality?
faq.a2: Some quality loss occurs because JPG uses lossy compression. At 85% quality (our default), the difference is barely visible to the human eye. You can adjust the quality slider from 10% to 100% to control the tradeoff.
faq.q3: How do I open AVIF files on Windows 10 or 11?
faq.a3: Windows 10 and 11 can open AVIF files if you install the free 'AV1 Video Extension' from the Microsoft Store. Alternatively, use Avifkit to convert AVIF to JPG and open the result in any photo viewer.
faq.q4: Can I open AVIF on iPhone or iOS?
faq.a4: iPhones running iOS 16 or later support AVIF natively in Safari and the Photos app. On older iOS versions, convert the file to JPG first using Avifkit for guaranteed compatibility.
faq.a5: macOS Ventura (13) and later support AVIF in Preview and Safari. On older versions of macOS, use Avifkit to convert AVIF files to JPG or PNG before opening them.
faq.a6: Chrome 85+, Firefox 93+, Edge 121+, Opera 71+, and Safari 16.4+ all support AVIF natively. For older browsers or apps, convert to JPG for universal compatibility.
faq.a7: AVIF generally produces files 30–50% smaller than JPG at the same visual quality. It also supports transparency, HDR, and animation — features JPG doesn't have. However, JPG has universal compatibility, which AVIF still lacks on older platforms.
faq.q8: Does AVIF to JPG conversion preserve transparency?
faq.a8: JPG does not support transparency. Transparent areas in your AVIF file will be filled with a white background when converted to JPG. If you need to preserve transparency, convert to PNG or WebP instead.
faq.q9: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
faq.a9: Each file can be up to 10 MB. Since all processing happens in your browser, larger files may take a moment longer depending on your device.
faq.q10: Can I convert multiple AVIF files at once?
faq.a10: Yes. Avifkit supports batch conversion of up to 30 files at a time. Drop all your files at once, click Convert All, and download them individually or as a ZIP archive.
